ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza food product class; no specific prosciutto-gorgonzola pizza subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific prosciutto-gorgonzola variant code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for prosciutto and gorgonzola pizza. No suitable parent code identified without risk of fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole pizza of approximately 350 g, typical of a single-serve Italian-style pizza as served at a restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat and vegetables, regular crust (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite); supplemented with USDA entries for prosciutto, gorgonzola, arugula, and olive oil
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for prosciutto-gorgonzola pizza. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cheese pizza (FDC ID 170919) as the base, adjusted upward for protein and fat to reflect prosciutto and gorgonzola DOP toppings, and cross-checked against USDA entries for prosciutto (FDC ID 167902) and gorgonzola-type blue cheese (FDC ID 173414). Arugula, peach, and balsamic reduction contribute minor macro adjustments. The resulting per-100g composite is a defensible estimate for a restaurant-prepared prosciutto-gorgonzola pizza.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; pizza entries including thin-crust and regular-crust cheese pizza
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for prosciutto-gorgonzola pizza. GI of 50 is estimated from peer-reviewed GI table entries for regular-crust cheese pizza (GI range 45–57, mean ~50) published in Atkinson et al. 2021 (Am J Clin Nutr). The high fat and protein content from prosciutto and gorgonzola is expected to attenuate glycemic response relative to a plain cheese pizza, supporting a GI at or below 50. GL per serving calculated as (50 × 27.5 g carbohydrate per 100g × 350g serving) / 100 = 48.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from prosciutto, gorgonzola DOP, and grana padano
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 620 mg sodium per 100 g (equivalent to ~2170 mg per whole pizza serving), this dish substantially exceeds the recommended single-meal sodium target of 600–800 mg for hypertensive individuals and should be consumed only occasionally with careful portion control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~50) with significant carbohydrate load from pizza dough
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the GI is moderate-to-low (~50) due to fat and protein from prosciutto and gorgonzola attenuating glucose absorption, the glycemic load per whole pizza is high (~48 g GL); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one or two slices and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from gorgonzola DOP, grana padano, and fior di latte, combined with high sodium from cured prosciutto
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of approximately 5.2 g saturated fat per 100 g and ~620 mg sodium per 100 g raises LDL-cholesterol and blood pressure risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to a small portion (1–2 slices) and choose lower-sodium accompaniments to stay within daily saturated fat (<20 g) and sodium (<1500 mg) targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prosciutto and Gorgonzola Pizza is an Italian-style pizza featuring a thin, baked wheat-flour crust topped with fior di latte (fresh cow's-milk mozzarella), 18-month aged prosciutto crudo, gorgonzola DOP (a protected-designation Italian blue cheese), fresh peach slices, wild arugula (rocket), a drizzle of balsamic reduction, extra virgin olive oil, and shaved grana padano. The combination of sweet peach, peppery arugula, salty cured ham, and pungent blue cheese creates a complex sweet-savoury-bitter flavour profile. The crust is crisp at the edges and slightly chewy at the centre. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense (~248 kcal per 100 g) with moderate protein (~11.5 g) from the prosciutto and cheeses, moderate-to-high fat (~10.8 g, of which ~5.2 g saturated) from the dairy and cured meat, and a significant carbohydrate contribution (~27.5 g) from the dough. Sodium content is high (~620 mg per 100 g) due to the prosciutto and aged cheeses, making it a concern for hypertensive and cardiovascular-disease patients. The glycemic index is estimated at ~50 (low-to-moderate), as the fat and protein content slows glucose absorption, but the glycemic load per whole pizza is substantial. This dish is not suitable for halal or kosher diets and contains gluten and dairy allergens. It is commonly served as a single-serve restaurant pizza in Italian and contemporary European dining contexts.

What is fior di latte on a pizza?
Glycemic index of pizza with cured meat and blue cheese

confidence_reason: No exact match for prosciutto-gorgonzola pizza exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza as a base, adjusted for prosciutto and gorgonzola toppings using individual USDA ingredient entries. GI is estimated from the closest peer-reviewed analogous food (regular-crust cheese pizza). Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA composite estimate for this pizza type and is not reliably quantified across the ingredient mix without a direct laboratory analysis. All other -1 fields (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) have been populated with estimated values from USDA FoodData Central composite data and are not sentinel -1 values — only vitamin_d_mcg remains at -1 as genuinely unreported. Food group retained as 'Grains and staples' consistent with input record, as pizza is dough-based. The input food_name 'Prosciutto & Gorgonzola' has been expanded to 'Prosciutto and Gorgonzola Pizza' to reflect the canonical dish identity. This dish is served at Mingle - Funan (field survey data) at SGD 36.90. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Italian dish; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese mandatory language rules apply. The romanized Italian name 'Pizza con Prosciutto e Gorgonzola' is provided as a romanized_local alias.
